event__initiative_information.name_of_proposed_initiative: Run for Recovery: Community 5K in Support of Calgary Health Trust
event__initiative_information.name_of_venue: Pearce Estate Park & Bow River Pathway
event__initiative_information.location_of_event: In-person outdoor event along the Bow River pathway system near Pearce Estate Park in southeast Calgary

event_description__financials.briefly_describe_the_event_and_how_the_f: Run for Recovery is a family-friendly 5K fun run and 2K walk along the Bow River pathways, organized by the East Calgary Community Wellness Alliance to raise funds for Calgary Health Trust in support of mental health and addiction recovery programs at local hospitals. Funds will be raised primarily through participant registration fees (adults, youth, and family packages), optional personal fundraising pages where participants can solicit pledges from friends and family, and day-of-event donations collected at an information and donation tent.

In addition to registrations and pledges, we will partner with two local coffee shops and a bakery that will donate a portion of their onsite sales from coffee, tea, and baked goods sold at the event. We will also have a small “wellness marketplace” area where a limited number of local vendors (e.g., yoga studios, physiotherapy clinics, and healthy snack companies) will pay a modest booth sponsorship fee in exchange for a table, with clear signage stating that their fees are supporting Calgary Health Trust. All sponsorship arrangements will be confirmed in writing and will specify that no hospital staff or resources are being used to solicit funds.

Leading up to the event, we will promote online donations through a simple website and social media campaign emphasizing personal stories of recovery within our community (shared only with explicit consent). Participants will be encouraged to set individual fundraising goals (e.g., $250 each) and share their personalized donation links. We will also work with a local running club to host two optional training runs, where participants can make a suggested $10 donation each time; these training runs will be fully volunteer-led and require minimal costs (water and light snacks, which are donated by a local grocery store).

To ensure transparency, we will track all income and expenses in a simple spreadsheet, keep receipts for any costs (such as race bibs, safety vests, and permits), and clearly communicate that all net proceeds after covering approved event expenses will be donated to Calgary Health Trust. We will not engage in door-to-door fundraising or telephone solicitations. All promotional materials will state “Proceeds in support of Calgary Health Trust” and will be submitted for approval before being printed or posted online. No portion of the funds will be used to compensate volunteers or organizers; everyone involved from our group is participating on a volunteer basis.
